screenSummary.playArea: The play area shows a flat map of Earth and a globe. A draggable observer marker sets one latitude and longitude, shown on both.
screenSummary.controlArea: The control area has latitude and longitude fields, a reference-circles toggle, and a Reset All button.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Drag the observer on the map or globe, or type a latitude or longitude, to explore Earth's coordinates.

terrestrial.currentDetails: The screen is in its initial state.

controls.mapFeatures: Show the tropic and polar reference circles and the International Date Line
controls.showCities: Label reference cities on the map
controls.globeObserverHelp: Drag to move the observer, or hold Shift and drag to rotate the globe. Use the arrow keys to nudge the observer's latitude and longitude.
controls.googleMaps: Open the current location in Google Maps
controls.useMyLocation: Set the observer to my current location

screenSummary.playArea: The play area shows a flat sky map and a celestial sphere. A draggable star sets one right ascension and declination, shown on both.
screenSummary.controlArea: The control area has right-ascension and declination fields, toggles for the grid, celestial equator, ecliptic, galactic equator, equinoxes and solstices, poles, and zodiac constellations, plus a coordinate-format toggle, map-shift buttons, and a Reset All button.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Drag the star on the map or the sphere, or type a right ascension or declination, to explore celestial coordinates.

celestial.currentDetails: The screen is in its initial state.
celestial.currentDetailsPattern: The star is at right ascension {{ra}} hours and declination {{dec}} degrees.

controls.flatSkyMapStarHelp: Drag or use the arrow keys to move the star. Right ascension increases to the left.
controls.celestialSphereHelp: Drag or use the arrow keys to rotate the sphere.
controls.guideStarHelp: Drag or use the arrow keys to move the star's right ascension and declination.

screenSummary.playArea: The play area shows Earth orbiting the Sun with a fixed tilted axis, the Sun riding the ecliptic on a celestial sphere, an Earth close-up with its day/night terminator, and a sunbeam panel.
screenSummary.controlArea: The control area has play/pause, a latitude control, readouts for the date, the Sun's declination and right ascension, and the noon Sun altitude, plus toggles and a Reset All button.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Play the animation or drag Earth around its orbit, and change the latitude, to see how the seasons arise.

seasons.currentDetails: The screen is in its initial state.
seasons.currentDetailsPattern: It is {{date}}. The Sun's declination is {{declination}} degrees; at latitude {{latitude}} degrees the noon Sun altitude is {{altitude}} degrees.

controls.earthHelp: Drag Earth around its orbit, or use the arrow keys, to change the date.

controls.dragPerspectiveHint: click and drag to change perspective
controls.dragEarthHint: click and drag Earth to change its position on the orbital path
controls.dragLatitudeHint: the red latitude circle marks the observer's latitude
